column3: Departs South Africa to Middle East by sea

column8: “SS DUCHESS OF BEDFORD passing through the Suez Canal after delivering troops to Egypt, 5 October 1940.” Keating, Geoffrey John / © IWM E 697

column5: "As I remember the story, my grandmother had given him a special soap, one that could be used with the salt water showers available on the troop ship.  When he landed, this soap was in his backpack.  During Sidi Reghez, probably, Dan felt a searing pain in his back. He turned around and saw a German soldier with rifle raised.  Dan then shot back.  When the firefight died down, Dan finally checked his backpack. In it was the soap, with a round embedded in it.  Funnily enough, some members of the family remember the protective item as a cigarette case, as if he were James Bond in From Russia with Love, or a hundred other novels and books.  But I know better, because I have the soap."
column7: As I remember the story, my grandmother had given him a special soap, one that could be used with the salt water showers available on the troop ship.  When he landed, this soap was in his backpack.  During Sidi Reghez, probably, Dan felt a searing pain in his back. He turned around and saw a German soldier with rifle raised.  Dan then shot back.  When the firefight died down, Dan finally checked his backpack. In it was the soap, with a round embedded in it.  Funnily enough, some members of the family remember the protective item as a cigarette case, as if he were James Bond in From Russia with Love, or a hundred other novels and books.  But I know better, because I have the soap.

column3: Ceses to be attached to 1 SA Inf Brigade
Admitted to Hospital 'Ithaca' on X2 List 6
Seconded to British Military Mission (BMM) to Polish forces
column5: "Dan was attached to the 2nd Polish Corps.  These were men who had been captured by the Soviets during their stab-in-the-back invasion of Poland during the German blitzkrieg in September 1939.  But after Operation Barbarossa, the German invasion of the Soviet Union in June 1940, Briitsh Prime Minister Winston Churchill had convinced the Soviets to release those troops to serve under British command.    My grandmother remembers them as needing “to go through a sheep dip” to get rid of the lice first. :

column8: "Date arrived in what is now Persia & Iraq Command 16 Sept 1942" [1]
Officers’ record entries: "A - MAJOR Date 16 NOV 1942" and "T - MAJOR Date 16 DEC 1942" [1]
Incorrectly filed with Jack Gonski records

column3: Posted to 26 BLU (26th British Liason unit) attached  2 Polish Corps
Attended XVI Course Officers Wing Middle East Theatre Command
column5: "Results: Application - A. Administrative  Knowledge - A. Administrative Ability - A."

column3: Permanently promoted to rank of  Captain
Granted leave, emplaned to Cairo to South Africa
Arrives in South Africa by air from Middle East
Placed on Marriage establishment, changes next-of-kin
Leaves South Africa by air for Middle East
Arrives in Italy with 2 Polish Corps
column8: Incorrectly filed with Jack Gonski records

column5: One day, it appears, my grandfather and his batman (servant) hiding in a bunker when a bomb dropped nearby.  The bomb, fortunately, did not go off.  The bomb disposal unit came, and carefully took the bomb apart.  But there was no detonator inside.  In its place was a note that said “we also fight the same enemy.  Your Czechoslovakian friend.”

column3: Major Polish assault at Monte Cassino

column2: By sea from Italy to Suez
column3: Placed on SC (V) G/L on cessation of secondment to UK forces.  Passing through Egypt, meets with his brother, Alec.
column5: "Allegedly, the two brothers gave a ride to two young Polish female soldiers – probably nurses. The women preceded to discuss the two men and their various attributes totally unaware that Jack and Grampa understood every word.  every word.  As my mother writes, “they were quite embarrassed at journey's end”."

column2: By sea from Suez to South Africa

column3: Date of discharge (partial demobilisation); awarding of  Colonel rank
